title: 11 Herbal Creams For Oily Face
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great solution for oily skin. These creams often contain herbs that help to balance the skin's natural oil production, reducing the appearance of shine and acne. So, why do herbal teas help with oily skin? The answer lies in their ability to calm and soothe the skin. When you drink herbal teas, the active ingredients are absorbed into the bloodstream, which then helps to reduce inflammation and regulate the skin's oil glands.  For example, Aloe barbadensis (also known as aloe vera) is a popular herb used in herbal creams for oily skin. It has anti-inflammatory properties that help to calm redness and irritation, while also providing hydration to the skin. Melaleuca alternifolia (also known as tea tree oil) is another herb that's often used in herbal creams. It has antibacterial properties that help to reduce acne and prevent further breakouts. Finally, Lavandula angustifolia (also known as lavender oil) is a calming herb that helps to reduce stress and promote a good night's sleep, which is essential for healthy skin.  When you use herbal creams with these herbs, you can expect to see a reduction in oil production, a decrease in acne, and an improvement in the overall health of your skin. These benefits can bring a lot of relief to people with oily skin, allowing them to feel more confident and comfortable in their own skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-juice, which are rich in vitamins and minerals. These components help to regulate the skin's natural oil production, reducing the appearance of oily skin. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloin and aloe-emodin soothe the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin. Aloe barbadensis also contains salicylic acid, which exfoliates the skin, unclogging pores and preventing oil buildup. By balancing the skin's natural pH and oil production, aloe barbadensis creams can help to control oily skin and promote a healthier complexion.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/4 cup of aloe vera gel, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil, and 2 teaspoons of vitamin E oil.
Combine the distilled water and aloe vera gel in a small saucepan. Heat over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is warm and well combined.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool for a few minutes. Add the coconut oil, jojoba oil, and vitamin E oil to the mixture. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar or container. Let it cool to room temperature before using. Store in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.
To use, apply a small amount to your oily face and massage it in. Use as needed throughout the day to control oil production and soothe the skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains active constituents like cineole, terpinen-4-ol, and limonene. These compounds have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help control oily skin. Cineole and terpinen-4-ol reduce the growth of bacteria that can cause acne, while limonene helps to dry up excess oil. Terpinen-4-ol also has a soothing effect that can calm redness and irritation. By regulating oil production and reducing inflammation, Melaleuca alternifolia creams can help to balance the skin's natural moisture levels.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until smooth.
Add beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until fully incorporated.
Remove from heat and stir in Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil.
Pour the mixture into a silicone mold or a small tin container and let cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams contains the active constituents linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce the production of sebum, the oily substance produced by the skin, thereby controlling oiliness. Additionally, the calming effect of linalool and linalyl acetate can soothe irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with oily skin. Lavandula angustifolia creams also contain antioxidants, such as beta-caryophyllene, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals, promoting a balanced and healthy complexion. By reducing inflammation and excess sebum production, Lavandula angustifolia creams can help to regulate oily skin and promote a more balanced skin tone.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup distilled water, 1/4 cup coconut oil, 1/4 cup shea butter, 2 tablespoons beeswax, 10 drops lavender essential oil.
Melt coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool slightly. Then, slowly add the distilled water while stirring constantly.
Continue stirring until the mixture thickens and reaches a creamy consistency. This may take about 10-15 minutes.
Add the lavender essential oil and stir well. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool before using on your oily face.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis creams contains essential oils rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The main active constituents in these creams are borneol, bornyl acetate, and camphor, which have antibacterial and antifungal properties. These properties help to regulate the skin's natural pH balance, reduce oil production, and prevent clogged pores. The astringent properties of rosmarinic acid also help to tighten the skin and reduce the appearance of pores, making it an effective treatment for oily skin. By balancing the skin's natural oil production and reducing inflammation, Rosmarinus officinalis creams can provide relief from oily face and promote a healthier complexion.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1/2 cup of shea butter, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 teaspoons of rosemary essential oil.
Melt shea butter, coconut oil, and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove from heat and stir in olive oil and rosemary essential oil until well combined.
Let the mixture cool and thicken for about 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a creamy consistency.
Pour the mixture into a clean, sterilized container and let it cool completely before use. Use on oily face as a moisturizer.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds work to reduce inflammation and calm the skin, making it an effective solution for oily faces. The anti-inflammatory properties of calendula help to soothe redness and irritation, while its antiseptic properties help to control oil production and prevent breakouts. The carotenoids in calendula also have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promote a balanced skin tone. By reducing inflammation and controlling oil production, calendula creams can help to regulate the skin's natural oil balance and leave oily faces looking clearer and more even.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Calendula officinalis flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Steep the Calendula flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 10-15 minutes, then strain and let cool.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the cooled Calendula infusion, shea butter, and vitamin E oil to the melted coconut oil and beeswax mixture. Stir until well combined.
Remove from heat and let cool until the mixture thickens. Pour into a jar and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents like glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, which help reduce oil production on the skin. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin, while flavonoids possess antioxidant properties that combat free radicals causing excess oil production. These compounds also have astringent properties, which help tighten pores and reduce sebum secretion, making it easier to control oily skin. Additionally, glycyrrhiza glabra has been shown to balance the skin's natural pH, preventing excessive oil production and reducing the appearance of pores. By regulating oil production and soothing the skin, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams provide a natural solution for managing oily skin.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1/4 cup of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of glycyrrhiza glabra root powder, 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel, and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil.
Combine distilled water and glycerin in a saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat until the glycerin dissolves.
Add glycyrrhiza glabra root powder to the saucepan and stir well. Remove from heat and let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids. Add aloe vera gel and jojoba oil. Mix well until combined.
Pour the mixture into a clean container. Let it cool and thicken before using it as a face cream for oily skin.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams contains the active constituents limonene, linalool, and geraniol, which are known for their astringent and antiseptic properties. These compounds help to control the production of sebum, the oily substance produced by the skin, thereby reducing the appearance of oily skin. The antiseptic properties of geraniol and linalool also help to prevent the growth of bacteria that can contribute to acne and other skin problems often associated with oily skin. Additionally, the astringent properties of limonene help to tighten the pores, reducing the appearance of large pores and giving the skin a smoother appearance. By controlling sebum production and preventing bacterial growth, Cymbopogon citratus creams can help to maintain a balanced and healthy complexion.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of Cymbopogon citratus essential oil, 1 tablespoon of vitamin E oil.
Combine aloe vera gel and coconut oil in a bowl. Stir well until combined.
Add Cymbopogon citratus essential oil and vitamin E oil to the bowl. Stir well to mix.
Pour the mixture into a clean jar or container. Let it cool and solidify.
Apply a small amount to your oily face, avoiding the eye area. Massage gently and let it absorb before applying makeup.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris creams contains thymol and carvacrol, which are active constituents that help balance oily skin. Thymol has antiseptic and astringent properties that reduce oil production and tighten pores, controlling sebum secretion. Carvacrol has antibacterial properties that prevent acne-causing bacteria from multiplying, reducing inflammation and breakouts associated with oily skin. Thymus vulgaris also contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant properties, protecting the skin from damage caused by free radicals and promoting a healthy skin balance. By reducing oil production and preventing bacterial growth, Thymus vulgaris creams can help regulate oily skin and prevent associated problems.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup distilled water, 1/4 cup coconut oil, 2 tablespoons beeswax, 2 tablespoons shea butter, 10 drops thyme essential oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter to the melted mixture and stir until combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool until it reaches 120°F to 130°F.
Add distilled water and thyme essential oil to the mixture, then whip until it becomes a smooth cream. Pour into a container and store in the fridge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis creams contains saponins and flavonoids as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds help to balance the skin's natural pH and reduce the production of sebum, making it a good option for oily faces. The saponins in Saponaria officinalis creams have astringent properties, which help to tighten the pores and prevent them from getting clogged with excess oil. Additionally, the flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation associated with oily skin. By regulating sebum production and reducing inflammation, Saponaria officinalis creams can help to keep oily skin under control.
remedies.image_alt: Saponaria officinalis herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of Saponaria officinalis flowers, 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Steep Saponaria officinalis flowers in coconut oil for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the mixture every day.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl, discard the flowers.
Melt beeswax and shea butter in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval until smooth.
Combine the infused coconut oil, melted beeswax mixture, and vitamin E oil in a bowl, stir until well combined and pour into a container to cool and solidify.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, caffeic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce redness and soothe skin irritations often associated with oily skin. The alkylamides in Echinacea purpurea have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which can contribute to acne and other skin issues. The antioxidants in the cream, including caffeic acid and rosmarinic acid, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and exacerbate oily skin conditions. By reducing inflammation and protecting skin cells, Echinacea purpurea creams can help to promote a healthier balance of oil production and reduce the appearance of oily skin.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of Echinacea purpurea tincture, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of lavender essential oil.
Melt beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl in 10-second increments, stirring between each interval, until fully melted.
Remove melted mixture from heat and stir in Echinacea purpurea tincture, vitamin E oil, and lavender essential oil until well combined.
Pour mixture into a clean, dry container and let cool until solidified. This may take about 30 minutes.
Once solidified, use a spoon to scoop out a small amount of the Echinacea cream and apply it to your oily face. Massage gently until absorbed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album creams contains essential oils, including santalol and alpha-bisabolol. These oils have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which help to reduce excess oil production on the skin. Santalol, in particular, is known for its ability to tighten pores and reduce sebum production, making it an effective ingredient for oily skin. Alpha-bisabolol also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to calm redness and irritation caused by excess oil. By balancing oil production and reducing inflammation, Santalum album creams can help to regulate oily skin and promote a healthier complexion.
remedies.image_alt: Santalum album herbal creams for oily face
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of jojoba oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Remove from heat and stir in the shea butter and jojoba oil until well combined and smooth.
Add 10-15 drops of Santalum album essential oil to the mixture and stir well. Start with 10 drops and adjust to your liking.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it cool and solidify at room temperature before use.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce oil production on your face. This is because some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have natural oils that can balance your skin's natural oil levels. Drinking these teas may help keep your pores clean and reduce the appearance of an oily face.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help balance your skin's oil production, but drinking them daily may not be the best approach. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, can dry out your skin, making it more oily in the long run. It's essential to find the right balance and not overdo it.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas like peppermint, chamomile, and green tea can help reduce oiliness on the face. It may take a few days to a week to notice a difference. As you drink the tea regularly, your body starts to balance its oil production. Some people may see results sooner, while others may take a bit longer.
faq_time: For an oily face, drinking herbal tea in the morning might help. This is because you're giving your skin a boost before the day gets hot and oily. Many herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have calming properties that can help regulate your skin's oil production.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications, including those for an oily face. Some herbs, like St. John's Wort, can affect how your body processes other medications. If you're taking medications for an oily face, be cautious with herbal teas like ginkgo biloba, which can also affect skin conditions.
faq_pregnant: If you have an oily face and are pregnant, it's best to be cautious when drinking herbal teas. Some teas, like dandelion or peppermint, can cause hormonal changes that might affect your pregnancy. However, teas like chamomile or lavender are generally considered safe. But it's still a good idea to check the ingredients and labels carefully before consuming them.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and have an oily face, consuming herbal teas might not be the best idea. Some herbal teas can stimulate your body to produce more oil, making your skin condition worse. It's also possible that the tea's active ingredients might pass into your milk, which could affect your baby.
title_2: Get the Glow Back: 11 Herbal Creams for Teenagers with Oily Face
